title: Sweet Aromatic Zhejiang Rice Congee
description: A comforting Chinese dish from Zhejiang province, known for its sweet and sour flavor, fluffy texture, and Mediterranean-friendly ingredients. This recipe is perfect for a cold winter morning.
instructions: Heat the sesame oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the sliced ginger and cook for 2-3 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the pork or vegetable stock and bring to a boil.
Add the glutinous rice, dark brown sugar, soy sauce, and rice vinegar.
Stir well to combine, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the rice is fluffy and the liquid has been absorbed.
Season with black pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped scallions.
Deglaze the saucepan with a small amount of water to scrape up any browned bits from the bottom.
Strain the congee into a serving bowl and discard the solids.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional scallions and sesame oil if desired.
